Name: tensor_core_kv_cache
Title: Tensor Core-Optimized KV Cache Layouts for Efficient LLM Inference
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging Tensor Cores and optimizing KV cache layouts for low-bit quantization, we can significantly reduce memory bandwidth and decoding latency in long-context LLM inference without sacrificing accuracy.
Related Work: Recent work like BitDecoding has explored low-bit KV cache quantization but focuses on CUDA cores. Spectral Quantization Routing and other training-free methods offer insights into precision allocation but lack hardware-specific optimizations. Our proposal uniquely combines Tensor Core utilization with optimized KV cache layouts, addressing a gap in hardware-efficient inference.
Abstract: The growing demand for long-context Large Language Models (LLMs) has highlighted the inefficiencies in memory bandwidth and decoding latency, particularly due to the Key-Value (KV) cache. While low-bit quantization offers a promising solution, existing methods primarily rely on CUDA cores, neglecting Tensor Cores—the primary compute resource on modern GPUs. We propose a Tensor Core-optimized approach to KV cache layouts, enabling efficient low-bit quantization by leveraging Tensor Cores for dequantization and memory access. Our method introduces warp-level parallelization strategies and optimized layouts to maximize Tensor Core utilization. We evaluate our approach on modern GPU architectures, measuring decoding speedup, memory bandwidth reduction, and accuracy retention. Preliminary results suggest a significant improvement in throughput and latency, making it a viable solution for real-world deployment.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Measure the impact of Tensor Core utilization on decoding speedup using low-bit (4-bit and 2-bit) KV cache quantization. Metrics: Tokens per second, p50/p99 latency.
Experiment 2: Compare memory bandwidth reduction between CUDA core-only and Tensor Core-optimized approaches. Metrics: Achieved bandwidth, HBM round-trips.
Experiment 3: Evaluate accuracy retention across different quantization levels and Tensor Core optimizations. Metrics: Perplexity, task-specific accuracy.
Experiment 4: Profile kernel performance to identify bottlenecks and optimize warp-level parallelization. Metrics: Occupancy, warp stall reasons, arithmetic intensity.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Risk 1: Tensor Core optimizations may not generalize across all GPU architectures.
Risk 2: Low-bit quantization could introduce accuracy degradation in certain tasks.
Limitation 1: The approach requires specific hardware support for Tensor Core operations.
Limitation 2: Optimized layouts may increase kernel complexity and development time.
